title: Moya Brennan: A Famous Irish Singer
summary: Moya Brennan was a famous Irish singer who passed away at age 73. She was best known as the lead singer of the group Clannad, which won awards like Grammys and Baftas. Moya came from a family in Donegal, Ireland, and started singing with her family in their pub. Clannad became popular worldwide, especially with songs for TV shows like Harry's Game and Robin of Sherwood. They were the first band to sing in Irish on a big TV music show. Moya also had a solo career and worked with other famous musicians. People loved her voice, and she was remembered as a kind person who loved her home in Donegal.

background_read: Moya Brennan was an Irish singer from Donegal who sang with her family in a group called Clannad. They became famous for songs in TV shows and won big awards. She also sang alone and with other stars. People admired her voice and her love for home.
Article_Structure: WHO: Moya Brennan, an Irish singer from Donegal
WHAT: She died at age 73 and was famous for singing in Clannad and solo
WHERE: She lived in Donegal, Ireland, and performed worldwide
WHY: She was remembered for her music and kind personality

perspectives.description: Moya Brennan helped bring Irish music to the world through Clannad and her solo work.
